You can use the pause timer to automatically pause videos or music that can be stopped with the spacebar at a specified time.

You can use the pause timer to automatically pause videos or music that can be stopped with the spacebar at a specified time.
You can automatically stop background music on platforms like YouTube or Audible using a timer or scheduled time.
Since it stops playback by simulating a spacebar press, it cannot be used on video or music streaming sites where the spacebar does not pause playback.

一時停止タイマーを使用すると、スペースバーで停止できるビデオや音楽を、指定した時間に自動的に一時停止することができます。

An autonomous AI agent that performs web browsing actions autonomously to complete a given goal/task (using LLM as brain).…

An autonomous AI agent that performs web browsing actions autonomously to complete a given goal/task (using LLM as brain).

Keyboard shortcut to open clickolas;

Windows/Linux: Ctrl+Shift+L
Mac: Command+Shift+L
Once the extension popup opens, you'll see a text area where you can enter your goal or task.
You can type your goal directly or use the speech-to-text feature:
Click the "Start Listening" button to activate speech recognition.
Speak your goal clearly.
Click "Stop Listening" when you're done.
Click the "Submit" button or press Enter to start the automation process.
A side panel will appear on the webpage, showing the steps Clickolas Cage is taking to complete your task.
You can interact with the side panel to provide additional input or stop the execution if needed.
API Keys OpenRouter API key
To use Clickolas Cage, you'll need to provide OpenRouter API key for the language models. The extension supports multiple providers:

Sidepanel Controls
Once Clickolas Cage starts executing your task, a sidepanel will appear on the right side of the webpage. This panel provides the following controls and information:

Minimize/Maximize: Click the '▼' or '▲' button in the top-right corner to minimize or maximize the sidepanel.

Drag and Move: You can click and drag the top bar of the sidepanel to reposition it on the page.

Message List: The main area of the sidepanel displays messages, including:

Steps being executed
User inputs
System messages
Completion messages
Stop Execution: When Clickolas Cage is actively executing a task, a "Stop Execution" button will appear. Click this to halt the current operation.

Input Area: When not executing, you'll see an input field where you can:

Type additional instructions or queries
Use voice input for hands-free interaction
Voice Input:

Click the "Voice" button or use the keyboard shortcut (Ctrl+Shift+K) to start/stop voice recognition.
When active, it will display "Stop" and the button will highlight.
Speak your instruction clearly, then click "Stop" or use the shortcut again to submit.
Send Button: Click "Send" or press Enter to submit your typed or voice-transcribed input.

The sidepanel allows you to monitor the progress of your task, provide additional input when needed, and control the execution of Clickolas Cage directly from the webpage.
Dims, mutes, speeds up (2x-16x) and auto-skips YouTube ads. Ads keep playing, so no ad-blocker detection!

🎬 YouTube Ad Dimmer - Dim Annoying Ads Respectfully

This extension dims, mutes, speeds up and auto-skips YouTube ads.
The ad keeps playing in the background - YouTube still receives all ad revenue!

✨ What does this extension do?
• Automatically detects YouTube ads
• Overlays the video with a black screen
• Mutes the audio
• Speeds up ads (2x, 4x, 8x, or 16x) - optional
• Auto-skips ads when the skip button appears
• YouTube still gets paid!

🎯 Perfect for:
Those repetitive, annoying ads you've seen a thousand times! 🙈

❓ Why not just use an adblocker?
This extension does NOT block ads - it only dims them. This means:
• YouTube creators still get paid
• YouTube receives ad revenue
• No ad-blocker detection warnings!
• You don't have to watch or listen

🔧 Features:
• On/Off toggle in popup
• Auto-Skip toggle (clicks "Skip Ad" button automatically)
• Speed slider (Off / 2x / 4x / 8x / 16x)
• Works in all YouTube views (Normal, Theater, Fullscreen)
• Settings are saved and synced across devices
• Live changes - adjust speed even during an ad!

⚠️ Good to Know:
• Auto-Skip uses Chrome's debugger to click the skip button. This briefly shows a debug bar at the top of the page (only for 1-2 seconds, then it disappears automatically).
• Higher speed may cause brief loading after the ad ends (YouTube needs time to buffer the main video).

📖 Open Source & Privacy:
• No data collection
• No external servers
• Everything runs locally in your browser

💡 Technical Note:
This is NOT an adblocker! Ads are not blocked - they play (just hidden).
YouTube's anti-adblocker measures don't apply here.

Have a feature request? Leave a review and let us know! ⭐

---

📋 Changelog:

🔹 Release 1.0
• Initial release
• Black screen overlay during ads
• Mutes audio during ads

🔹 Update 1.1
• Changed language to English
• Added "Rate us" button in popup

🔹 Update 1.2
• Added Auto-Skip feature (automatically clicks skip button)
• Added Speed slider (Off / 2x / 4x / 8x / 16x)
• Controls are disabled (greyed out) when extension is off
• Status display shows active features (Dimmed + Skip + 4x)
• Improved ad detection
• Settings sync across devices
• Live speed adjustment during ads

🚀 What's Next?
I'm working on YouTube Shorts ad dimming. Due to YouTube's dynamic UI, this is proving to be challenging - but I'm on it! Stay tuned.

Instant context-aware AI replies on social media posts. Works with LinkedIn, X, Slack, WhatsApp, Facebook, Instagram, and more

Postply generates customizable AI responses while considering the post text and other comments. It also understands context from images and memes. 

You can choose the style and length of the reply easily. The reply is not sent automatically, you'll have a chance to edit it first.

Postply is designed for those who want to grow their audience, but not spend hours crafting social media replies. Increase your account visibility easily by posting more often with AI Replies.

You can also customize your sender profile to generate replies as a business or brand.

## Key Features

- Context-Aware AI - Understands post content and conversation flow
- Multiple Styles - Agree, neutral, or disagree responses + LinkedIn, Reddit, 4Chan reply styles. Customizable on request.

## How It Works

- Step 1: Right-Click Any Post
Navigate to any social media post on a supported platform.

- Step 2: Choose Your Style and Reply Length
Right-click in the input field, and select "Postply -> Settings" from the context menu, then choose:

- Step 3: Get Your Reply
Right-click in the input field, and select "Postply -> Reply" from the context menu.
Postply analyzes the post content and generates a context-aware reply. The AI understands:
- Conversation context and history
- Your chosen stance (agree/neutral/disagree)
- Professional vs casual communication styles

- Step 4: Customize & Send
Review the generated reply, make any edits if needed, and send it.

## See our website for a list of supported social media platforms, let us know which platform you want added next.

## Perfect For

- Content Creators - Engage with your audience faster with AI replies
- Social Media Managers - Handle brand engagement efficiently
- Leadership teams - Use it to spark engagement on internal comms
- Business Professionals - Maintain active presence
- Community Managers - Respond to comments at scale
- Anyone - Who wants to save time on social media replies and grow account visibility.

Stop wasting time on manual replies. Start engaging smarter with Postply.

Changelist:
- "Reply As" profile support added (under Extension Options)
- WhatsApp bug fix
- Added support for MS Teams and Meta Threads
- Rebranded from Hotslop to Postply
- Added new settings context menu, for quick convenient configuration.
- You can now choose reply length: Short, Medium, Long

This extension analyzes the size of buttons, links, etc. on a smartphone web screen and displays the success rate of taps.

This extension analyzes the size of buttons, links, etc. on a smartphone web screen and displays the success rate of taps.
It was inspired by "Tappy" provided by Yahoo Japan Lab.

The tool has been created to help you to track the cases you are working with, and to avoid the use of external tools that can take you even more time.

When the extension has been installed, you will see a new bar menu at the top of the site, where you will have all the options you need to have track of your case.

When the selection of the options has been done and as soon as you click submit, all the selections will be stored in your clipboard. This will let you paste it later on, into your tracker**.

Once you click submit button, the tool will reset and let you select your next set of options for your following case.

The data copied to your clipboard will be the same data you chose, with a character that will work as a separator ("{", ":", "|"). The idea is to later on we will be able to split the info with those separators. That's why if you type any of these 3 separators characters, you will see a floating message that alerts you that you have typed one of these. Don't worry, you can go back to the text input options and delete them, or replace them.

**NOTE: If you don't paste the data on your tracker right next you hit the submit button, the information might get lost because any of that information isn't stored internally or externally (Like a database).

Simulate arrow keys and text editing keys using vim-style bindings.

Simulate arrow keys and text editing keys using vim-style bindings.

Since vimput simulates the browser's built-in text editing keys, it should natively work on all websites which allow arrow key navigation. This includes websites like Google Docs, which implement their own custom text rendering, websites like notion, where text is split across elements, and even websites where you are editing more than text, such as google sheets.

By default, the extension starts in "off" mode, and switches to "insert" mode whenever a text field is selected (but this behavior can be customized). Since Escape is already used by the browser, Alt+q (or Control+q for MacOS) is the default keybinding to enter normal mode. Once in normal mode, you will be able to use many of vim's default keybindings.

===== Supported Commands =====

Currently supported normal mode commands are:

Numeric prefixes for repeating any command
h/j/k/l for basic navigation
w/e/b for wordwise navigation
$/^/0 to jump to the start/end of the line
g/G to jump to the start/end of the document
d/c/y to read a motion, then perform a delete/change/copy
D/C/Y to perform a delete/change/copy on the current line
i/a to enter insert mode
I/A to enter insert mode at the start/end of the line
o/O to open a new line below/above, and then entering insert mode
s/S to delete the current character/line and enter insert mode
x/X to delete forward/backward
z/Z to delete a word forward/backward
u/U to undo/redo
J to merge the next line with the current line
v to enter visual mode
V to select the current line and enter visual mode
Currently supported visual mode commands are:

h/j/k/l/w/e/b/$/^/0/g/G to move the selection
d/c/y to delete/change/copy the selected text
o to swap the selection direction (visual mode)
q/A-q to exit visual mode at the spot of the cursor
i/a to enter insert mode at the beginning/end of the selection
The full list of keybindings and settings can be viewed and customized in the options menu once the extension is installed.

===== Disabling the debug popup =====

In order to simulate native keybindings, vimput must use chrome's debugger api. This has the delightful feature of providing you with a popup at the top of your screen every time the extension is used. In order to disable this popup, you must run chrome with the --silent-debugger-extension-api flag. See this article on how to run chrome with custom flags:
https://www.chromium.org/developers/how-tos/run-chromium-with-flags/

For brave users: This flag does not seem to work on the standard version of brave, but it does work on the beta version.

===== Macos limitations =====

MacOS does not allow simulating any events which have the Command prefix. Luckily, this only eliminates the beginning/end of file keys, and command shortcuts (cut, copy, paste, undo redo.) Due to this, d is mapped to Backspace on MacOS instead of Cut.

Also, MacOS will sometimes infer Option+key as a special character instead of the raw key combination.

===== Implementation =====

The extension is implemented by intercepting all keyboard events and replacing them with new ones. For example, if you press the l key while in normal mode, the extension will intercept it, and then send the page an ArrowRight event in its place. Similarly, if you were in visual mode, it would send a Shift+ArrowRight event in order to expand the selection to the right.

This should give a general idea of what the extension is and is not capable of. For example, it is possible to simulate 0 by pressing Home, but there is no way to simulate the ^ key, since the browser does not have a key to jump to the beginning of a line's text. The extension then implements repeated keys by simply performing the associated key presses a bunch of times.

The motion/operator system is a special case. When you press an operator, the extension will wait for the user to input a valid motion. The motion will then be run first, and select a region of text. The operator will then act on the selected text. For example, if you press dd, the extension will send Home to go to the beginning of the line, Shift+End to select to the end of the line, and finally Control+x to cut that region. Thus, if you wanted to remap d to simply delete instead of cut, you could write nmap d operator Backspace in your config. Since Backspace deletes whatever text is selected, it will work as an operator.

===== Configuration =====

Almost every aspect of the extension can be configured in the configuration file, which can be accessed by clicking on the extension icon. You can also provide site-specific configuration, including disabling the extension entirely on certain sites.
Intercept and simulate browser network requests. Customize API responses for frontend development without backend dependencies.

Crayfish Mock: Browser API Response Simulator
Purpose
Crayfish Mock is a powerful browser extension designed for frontend developers that intercepts and simulates browser network requests. It allows you to customize API responses, status codes, and introduce network delays without modifying your application code.
Why You Should Install It
Accelerate Development: Test your frontend against various API responses without waiting for backend implementation
Simulate Edge Cases: Easily create error scenarios, slow networks, or specific API behaviors
Work Offline: Continue development without relying on actual backend services
Consistent Testing: Create reproducible test scenarios with predefined responses
Debug with Ease: Troubleshoot frontend issues by isolating backend variables
Perfect for frontend developers who need reliable API mocking capabilities directly in their browser.

Shripi captures HTTP/HTTPS API requests from your browser tabs so you can debug, document, and replay them—without leaking secrets.

  ► CAPTURE
  • Record requests from the active tab or all tabs
  • Start and stop with one click; list auto-refreshes while capturing
  • Session-only or persistent storage (up to 1,000 entries)
  • Exclude URLs by regex pattern

  ► REDACT BEFORE STORAGE & EXPORT
  • Mask authorization headers, sensitive cookies, and query params with regex
  • Customize patterns for header names, cookie names, and body/query fields
  • Redaction applies at capture time and carries through every export format

  ► SEARCH, FILTER & INSPECT
  • Full-text search across Domain, URL, URL + Headers, or URL + Headers + Body
  • Filter by method (GET/POST/PUT/PATCH/DELETE/HEAD/OPTIONS/…), status (1xx–5xx), and time window (last 5 min / 30 min / this session)
  • Click any request for full detail: URL, headers, request and response bodies, cookies, and query params

  ► COPY AS CODE
  • Copy any request as cURL, JavaScript fetch(), or Python requests. More languages will be supported in future releases.
  • All copies respect your redaction settings

  ► EXPORT
  • JSON, Markdown (free) — CSV, Postman Collection v2.1, HAR 1.2 (Pro)
  • Clean mode: strip browser-only headers before export
  • Debug bundle (ZIP, Pro): summary.md, requests.json, and a replay.sh with curl commands; choose redacted or raw

  ► SUMMARY & PAGE SPEED
  • Status breakdown with error rate (4xx + 5xx)
  • Top 10 endpoints by request count; fastest and slowest requests
  • Core Web Vitals per page: TTFB, FCP, LCP, DOM Content Loaded, Load—color-coded good/needs improvement/poor
  • Export the full summary as a Markdown report

  ► VIEW MODES
  • Flat list or grouped by domain → endpoint (Pro)

  Data is processed and stored locally in your browser. No request history is sent to our servers.

Scan, filter, and download page images with advanced extraction, overlay cleanup, and ZIP export.

Unshackle  helps you collect images from webpages without manual source hunting.
It can scan normal image tags, responsive sources (srcset/picture), CSS backgrounds, blob:/data: images, canvas snapshots, and inline SVG. 🖼️
Filter by size/type, sort results, preview items, batch-select, rename, and download individually or as a ZIP. 📦
Overlay cleanup tools (preview, remove, undo, keyword-based cleanup) help unblock pages where popups or masks hide images. 🧹
Network capture can pick up images loaded dynamically after page actions, and built-in duplicate removal reduces repeated files before export. ⚡
Site access is requested only when you run scan/capture actions. This safe build focuses on image workflows, some functions are locked in this version 🔒
Unshackle is open source. The full open-source version on GitHub includes more features than this Chrome Web Store safe build.

Convert any webstite to editable Figma design. Requires the associated Figma plugin

Web to Canvas lets you convert any website or component into an editable Figma design.

Instead of taking screenshots or rebuilding layouts manually, you can capture a webpage and turn it into structured design layers. This makes it easier to study layouts, reuse structures, or start new designs based on real websites.

Web to Canvas works on most modern websites and can also capture interfaces generated by AI tools.

How it works:

1. Install the Web to Canvas Chrome extension
2. Open any website in your browser
3. Click the extension and choose what you want to capture:
   * Full page
   * A specific section or component
4. The extension converts the page and downloads a `.w2c` capture file
5. Open the Web to Canvas Figma plugin
6. Import the `.w2c` file to generate an editable Figma design

Features: 

* Capture full webpages and convert them into Figma designs
* Capture specific sections or components from any website
* Convert HTML layouts into editable design layers
* Works with most websites and modern web applications
* Capture pages generated by AI tools
* Export captures as .w2c files for the Web to Canvas Figma plugin
* Supports responsive layouts and common web components

Why people use Web to Canvas: 

Designers and developers use Web to Canvas to:

* Recreate website layouts quickly inside Figma
* Study how real websites are structured
* Collect design references and inspiration
* Start new designs using existing layouts as a base

Note: 
To accurately map website to Figma, we use Chrome's debugger API solely to resolve fonts and capture hover states, no browsing data is collected or transmitted.

A text expander that works across all text input contexts including Google Docs and Gmail

### What is OpenClip?

OpenClip is a powerful and lightweight text expander Chrome extension that lets you define shortcuts (triggers) that automatically expand into longer text snippets. Type a short trigger like `/sig` and instantly expand it to your full email signature, meeting notes template, or any frequently-used text.

**Works everywhere you type:**
- Standard input fields and textareas
- Gmail compose window
- Google Docs (including the canvas-based editor)
- Any contenteditable field across the web

### Core Features

**Text Expansion Made Simple**
- Define custom text snippets with short triggers (e.g., `/thanks` → "Thank you for reaching out")
- Customize the trigger prefix (default: `/`)
- Organize snippets into categories with color-coded organization
- Tag snippets for easy discovery and filtering
- Full-text search to find snippets quickly

**Smart Dynamic Fields**
- **Auto-Replace:** `~datestamp`, `~timestamp`, `~datetime` automatically insert current dates/times
- **Interactive Text Input:** Ask for user input when expanding (e.g., `<text label="Name">`)
- **Dropdown Selection:** Choose from predefined options (e.g., `<dropdown options="High, Medium, Low">`)
- **Date Picker:** Insert formatted dates with various format options
- **Math Calculator:** Perform calculations referencing other fields (e.g., `<math formula="{field-1} * 0.08" prefix="$">`)

**Intelligent Suggestions**
- Real-time autocomplete dropdown shows matching snippets as you type
- See trigger, content preview, and category at a glance
- Toggle autocomplete on/off in settings

**Multi-Device Sync**
- **Chrome Sync:** Snippets automatically sync across all your Chrome browsers signed into your Google account (100KB limit)
- **Local File Backup:** Optionally sync to a JSON file in your Google Drive, Dropbox, or iCloud folder for full backup and multi-computer access
- Auto-sync with configurable backup location

**Gamification & Stats**
- Track total expansions and characters saved
- See time saved compared to manual typing
- Build daily streaks and compete with yourself
- Unlock achievements like "Snippet Master" (500 expansions)
- View your top 5 most-used snippets
- Monitor your efficiency improvements over time

**Premium User Experience**
- Optional sound feedback on expansion (satisfying click sound)
- Clean, modern settings UI with organized tabs
- No ads, no tracking, no analytics
- 100% free and open source
Capture screenshots and create professional device mockups for presentations and social media

📸 Beautiful screenshots with device mockups, annotations & instant export. Free. Built by Novato.

FULL DESCRIPTION:

📸 Screen Lab is the ultimate screenshot tool for anyone who wants their work to look stunning — right from the browser, in seconds.

Built by Novato, a product studio focused on crafting sharp, no-nonsense tools for designers and developers, Screen Lab is everything you wish your screenshot workflow already was. No bloated software. No subscriptions. Just clean, beautiful results.

---

Your screenshots instantly go from raw captures to publish-ready visuals.

---

📁 Client presentations

Screen Lab is completely free. No premium plan. No watermarks. No feature locks. Every tool, every mockup, every export format — all yours, forever.

Screen Lab is a reflection of that philosophy: simple on the surface, powerful underneath.

Capture regions, full screen, and seamless scrolling long screenshots.

# SnapLet - Element & Area Screenshot Tool

**Capture any web element or drag-to-select region in one click.**

SnapLet is a powerful Chrome extension that revolutionizes how you capture screenshots on the web. Whether you need to grab a specific element, select a custom area, or document web content for presentations, SnapLet makes it effortless and precise.

## 🚀 Why Choose SnapLet?

### **Precision at Your Fingertips**
- **Element-Perfect Capture**: Hover over any web element to highlight it, then right-click to capture exactly what you need
- **Custom Area Selection**: Drag to select any rectangular region on the page with pixel-perfect accuracy
- **No More Cropping**: Say goodbye to taking full-page screenshots and manually cropping them later

### **Streamlined Workflow**
- **One-Click Operation**: Enable the tool once, then capture multiple elements without reopening the extension
- **Instant Downloads**: Screenshots are automatically saved to your Downloads folder with timestamped filenames
- **Visual Feedback**: Real-time highlighting shows exactly what will be captured before you click

### **Professional Quality**
- **High-Resolution Output**: Captures elements at their native resolution for crisp, clear images
- **Clean Boundaries**: Automatically detects element boundaries for perfect framing
- **Consistent Results**: Every screenshot maintains the original quality and formatting

## 🎯 Perfect For

### **Web Developers & Designers**
- Document UI components and design elements
- Create visual bug reports with precise element captures
- Build design systems and component libraries
- Capture responsive design variations

### **Content Creators & Marketers**
- Create tutorials and how-to guides
- Document user interfaces for presentations
- Capture social media posts and web content
- Build visual documentation and reports

### **Researchers & Students**
- Document web-based research findings
- Capture data visualizations and charts
- Create visual citations and references
- Build comprehensive web content archives

### **Quality Assurance & Testing**
- Document software bugs with precise screenshots
- Create test case documentation
- Capture different states of web applications
- Build visual regression test suites

## ✨ Key Features

### **🎯 Element Screenshot Mode**
- **Smart Highlighting**: Hover over any element to see exactly what will be captured
- **Right-Click Capture**: Simple right-click context menu integration
- **Automatic Detection**: Intelligently identifies clickable elements, images, text blocks, and containers
- **Boundary Recognition**: Captures complete elements including padding and borders

### **📐 Area Screenshot Mode**
- **Drag-to-Select**: Click and drag to select any rectangular area
- **Visual Selection Box**: Real-time preview of the selected area
- **Flexible Sizing**: Select areas as small as a few pixels or as large as the entire viewport
- **Confirmation Controls**: Press Enter to confirm or ESC to cancel selection

### **🌍 Multi-Language Support**
- **4 Languages**: English, Chinese (Simplified), French, and Japanese
- **Localized Interface**: All tooltips and messages in your preferred language
- **Automatic Detection**: Uses your browser's language settings by default

### **⌨️ Intuitive Controls**
- **ESC to Exit**: Double-tap ESC key to quickly exit any mode
- **Visual Hints**: On-screen instructions guide you through each step
- **Keyboard Shortcuts**: Efficient workflow with minimal mouse clicks

### **🔧 Advanced Technology**
- **Chrome DevTools Integration**: Uses Chrome's native screenshot API for maximum quality
- **Memory Efficient**: Lightweight design that doesn't slow down your browsing
- **Cross-Site Compatible**: Works on any website, including complex web applications

## 🚀 Getting Started

### **Installation**
1. Install SnapLet from the Chrome Web Store
2. Click the SnapLet icon in your browser toolbar
3. Choose your preferred capture mode
4. Start capturing!

### **Element Screenshot**
1. Click "Element Screenshot" in the popup
2. Hover over any web element to highlight it
3. Right-click and select "📸 Capture This Element"
4. Your screenshot is automatically saved!

### **Area Screenshot**
1. Click "Area Screenshot" in the popup
2. Click and drag to select your desired area
3. Press Enter to confirm or ESC to cancel
4. Your custom area screenshot is saved!

## 💡 Pro Tips

### **Maximize Your Efficiency**
- **Batch Capturing**: Enable element mode once, then capture multiple elements without reopening
- **Precise Selection**: Use area mode for capturing specific portions of complex layouts
- **Quick Exit**: Double-tap ESC to quickly exit any mode and return to normal browsing

### **Best Practices**
- **Wait for Load**: Ensure pages are fully loaded before capturing for best results
- **Check Highlights**: Verify the highlight covers your intended element before right-clicking
- **Use Area Mode**: For elements that don't highlight properly, use area selection instead

## 🔒 Privacy & Security

- **No Data Collection**: SnapLet doesn't collect, store, or transmit any personal data
- **Local Processing**: All screenshot processing happens locally in your browser
- **Minimal Permissions**: Only requests necessary permissions for screenshot functionality
- **Open Source Ready**: Clean, auditable codebase for transparency

### **Compared to External Tools**

Overview :

Use Docify Web to save any webpage as a high-quality PDF instantly. Whether you need to capture a whole page, a…

Overview :

Use Docify Web to save any webpage as a high-quality PDF instantly. Whether you need to capture a whole page, a specific section, or a clean text-only version, this extension makes it simple to keep your content for offline reading and sharing.

Docify Web is a reliable tool for anyone who needs to download web content for research, archiving, or work. It uses advanced technology to ensure your files look professional and stay organized.

🔥 Key Features of Docify Web :

Convert web content exactly the way you want with these powerful tools:

✅ Capture Entire Page PDF: Capture the entire length of a website in one clear, searchable file.
✅ Select Section PDF: Hover over any part of a page and click to save only that specific area with a clean border and perfect padding.
✅ Reader Mode PDF: Strip away ads, images, and sidebars to create a "book-style" document that is easy to read.
✅ Link to PDF: Paste a URL into the extension and generate a PDF without even having to open the page in your active tab.

💡 How to Use Docify Web

Getting started is easy! Here is how it works:

1️⃣ To save a full page: Just open the extension and click "Capture Entire Page"
2️⃣ To save a section: Click "Select Page Section," hover over the part you want, and left-click.
3️⃣ To use Reader Mode: Click "Reader Mode PDF" to instantly clean up the text and save it.
4️⃣ To save from a link: Click "Link to PDF," paste your link, and hit "Generate."

📄 Tips for Best Results

- Wait for the page to finish loading completely before you start the conversion.
- For the cleanest look, use Reader Mode on articles or blog posts to remove clutter.
- If you only need one paragraph or a specific image, use the Select Section tool to avoid extra pages.
- Remember that the "Link to PDF" feature might take a few extra seconds to load the page in the background.
 
💡 Why Choose Docify Web?
Saving a webpage as a PDF has many advantages over just taking a screenshot:
◆ Selectable Text: You can copy, paste, and highlight text within the PDF.
◆ Active Links: All links in the document remain clickable for future reference.
◆ Searchable: You can use "Ctrl+F" to find specific words inside your saved files.
◆ Offline Access: Read your saved articles on a plane or anywhere without internet.
◆ Small File Size: PDFs are often smaller and easier to share than high-resolution images.

🧑‍🎓 Who is Docify Web For?
- This tool is designed to help everyone stay productive:
- Students: Save research papers and articles to cite them later.
- Professionals: Archive receipts, terms of service, or data reports for documentation.
- Writers: Save inspiration and references in a clean, readable format.


⏳ Keep Your Web Content Organized
Stop worrying about bookmarks that lead to broken links or pages that get deleted. With Docify Web, you can turn any internet page into a permanent file on your computer. Keep your research, recipes, and records organized and secure with just a few clicks.
